title: Input structs for message_query and message_create
description:
What: Define MessageQueryInput and MessageCreateInput structs for the two highest-argument methods; update signatures and call sites; remove any remaining #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)].

Algorithm:

PART 1 — Define structs in methods.rs (before the impl block):
pub struct MessageQueryInput<'a> {
    pub chat_id: Option<&'a str>,
    pub has_mention: Option<bool>,
    pub has_attachment: Option<bool>,
    pub position: Option<u64>,
    pub limit: Option<u64>,
}
pub struct MessageCreateInput<'a> {
    pub client_id: &'a str,
    pub chat_id: &'a str,
    pub body: &'a str,
    pub body_type: &'a str,
    pub sent_at: &'a str,
    pub reply_to: Option<&'a str>,
}

PART 2 — Update signatures in methods.rs:
- message_query(&self, session: &Session, input: &MessageQueryInput<'_>) -> Result<QueryResponse, ClientError>
- message_create(&self, session: &Session, input: &MessageCreateInput<'_>) -> Result<SetResponse<serde_json::Value>, ClientError>
  Note: W1B will have already removed api_url and account_id; this bead adds the struct wrappers.
- Remove #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] from both methods.

PART 3 — Update call sites in client_task.rs:
Every client.message_query(&session, ...) call becomes:
  client.message_query(&session, &MessageQueryInput { chat_id: ..., has_mention: None, has_attachment: None, position: None, limit: Some(100) }).await?
Every client.message_create(&session, ...) call becomes:
  client.message_create(&session, &MessageCreateInput { client_id: &client_id, chat_id: &chat_id, body: &body, body_type: "text/plain", sent_at: &sent_at, reply_to: None }).await?

PART 4 — Update call sites in methods_test.rs:
  Same pattern — replace positional args with struct literals.

PART 5 — Check client_task.rs try_mark_read #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]:
  After W1B removes api_url/account_id params, count remaining args. If count <= 7, remove the allow. If still > 7, create a MarkReadInput struct.

Defensive rules:
- Structs use lifetime parameter 'a for borrowed strings — do not allocate.
- Default is NOT implemented on these structs (no default position/limit — callers must be explicit).
- All existing behavior is preserved — struct fields map 1:1 to old positional parameters.

Tests: cargo test --workspace passes. No #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] remain in methods.rs.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace passes. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes with no suppressions in methods.rs. MessageQueryInput and MessageCreateInput structs are public.
